input.instruction: Assess whether the evidence package has the gap this pattern describes. If the package is complete for this COU, the correct disposition is to accept — do NOT over-act on an alarming-looking but adequately-addressed signal. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Validation error present with documented directionality.

measures.cou_decision: stress must stay below a limit (over-prediction is conservative)

measures_raw.cou_decision_direction: stress must stay below a limit

case_context.cou: the prediction feeds a stress-margin decision

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The 12% error is documented one-sided conservative (always over-predicts the safety-relevant stress), so for a stay-below-limit decision it adds margin; the magnitude alarms but the direction makes it accounted-for.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.justification: 12% error is one-sided conservative (over-predicts stress) in the safety-relevant direction

forbidden_claims: rejects/restricts on the error magnitude while ignoring the documented conservative direction (over-action)
asserts the surrogate is accurate (a verdict the package does not adjudicate)

hardness.obvious_signal: the validation error looks large (12%) — well above a typical tolerance
hardness.conflicting_signal: the error is documented as one-sided conservative: the surrogate always OVER-predicts stress (the safety-relevant direction), by 12% at most, so for a stress-margin decision the error adds margin rather than eroding it

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40: error that is documented conservative in the decision-relevant direction is accounted-for; over-acting on its magnitude is the over-action a control must catch.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
